T1.instruction: Display a list of TODO tasks showing: title, status (done/pending), priority (high/medium/low), and due date.

T2.instruction: Show the detail view for a single task with all fields: title, description, status, priority, due date, tags, created and updated timestamps.

T3.instruction: Create a form for adding a new task with fields: title (required text), description (textarea), priority (select: low/medium/high, default medium), due date (date picker). Include a submit button labeled 'Create Task'.

T4.instruction: Update the existing task list: (1) add a new task titled 'New urgent task' with status pending and priority high, (2) change task #3's status from pending to done.
T4.dataDescription: Current task list (10 items) + change instructions
T4.note: For AFS-UI this is an incremental patch; for others it requires full regeneration

T5.instruction: Show the same 10-task dataset in three different layouts: (1) vertical list view, (2) card grid view, (3) table view with columns.
T5.dataDescription: 10 TODO items, rendered 3 times in different layouts

T6.instruction: Display the task list with a search box (filter by title text) and a status filter dropdown (all/pending/done). Show result count.

T7.instruction: Create a 3-step wizard for creating a new project: Step 1: project name (required) and description. Step 2: select team members from a list. Step 3: review selections and confirm. Include back/next navigation and a step indicator.

T8.instruction: Create a dashboard with 4 panels: (1) summary stat cards showing total, completed, pending, and overdue counts, (2) recent activity feed showing the last 5 actions, (3) priority distribution chart/breakdown, (4) abbreviated task list.
T8.dataDescription: Dashboard stats + 8 activity entries + priority breakdown + 10 tasks

T9.instruction: Create an interactive data table with: sortable column headers, pagination (5 items per page), row checkboxes for selection, a 'Select All' checkbox, a 'Mark Done' bulk action button (disabled when no rows selected), and an edit button per row.

T10.instruction: Display a chat message feed for channel '#afs-dev' with: sender name, timestamp, message text, and a message input area with a Send button at the bottom.

T10b.instruction: A new message has arrived in the chat: sender 'eve', text 'Evaluation results look great!', timestamp '2026-03-24T10:00:00Z'. Update the existing chat feed to include this message.
T10b.note: For AFS-UI this is a single patch op; for others it requires full regeneration
